gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/347)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/347)

parts.text: أَبِي الْخَزْرَجِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الزِّبْرِقَانِ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ

text: أَرْسَلَ إِلَيَّ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ نَحْنُ بِمِنًى ائْتِنِي وَ مَعَكَ كِنْفُكَ قَالَ فَأَتَيْتُهُ فِي مِضْرَبِهِ فَسَلَّمْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَرَدَّ عَلَيَّ وَ أَوْمَأَ إِلَيَّ أَنِ اجْلِسْ فَجَلَسْتُ ثُمَّ تَنَاوَلَ نَعْلًا جَدِيداً فَرَمَى بِهَا إِلَيَّ فَلَمَّا أَرَدْتُ أَنْ أَذْهَبَ قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ لَوْ وَهَبْتَ لِي هَذِهِ النَّعْلَ وَ كُنْتُ أَحْذُو عَلَيْهَا فَرَمَى إِلَيَّ بِالْفَرْدِ الْآخَرِ فَقَالَ وَاحِدَةٌ أَيُّ شَيْ‏ءٍ تَنْفَعُكَ
قَالَ وَ كَانَتْ مُعَقَّبَةً مُخَصَّرَةً مِنْ وَسَطِهَا لَهَا قِبَالَانِ وَ لَهَا رُءُوسٌ فَقَالَ هَذَا حَذْوُ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Abu Al Khazraj Al Hassan Bin Al Zabarqan Al Ansary, from Is’haq Al Haza’a who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> sent for me and we were at Mina: ‘Come to me<sup>asws</sup> and with you are your tools’. So I went over to him<sup>asws</sup> in his<sup>asws</sup> tent. I greeted him<sup>asws</sup> and he<sup>asws</sup> responded to me, and gestured to me that I should be seated. So I sat down. Then he<sup>asws</sup> gave me a new sandal, and gave it at me. So when I wanted to go, I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup> ! If only you<sup>asws</sup> would gift these sandals to me, and I can replicate upon this’. So he<sup>asws</sup> gave with the other one, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘One, which benefit can it give you?’

He (the narrator) said, ‘And it was such that it has a support for the heel, narrow from its middle, and for it were two straps, and for it were heads. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘This is a shoe (footwear) of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 7. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from abu al-Khazraj al-Hassan ibn al-Zabarqan al-Ansariy who has said that narrated to me Ishaq al-Hadhdha’ who has said the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, sent for me, when we were in Mina, to come to him with my tools. I went to his tent and offered him (the Imam) the greeting of peace to which he responded and made a hand gesture for me to sit down. I sat down. He (the Imam) took a new shoe and threw it to me. When I wanted to leave I said, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, I wish you gave this sandal to me as a present so I can use it as a model. He (the Imam) threw the other pair to me saying, ‘How can you benefit from one pair?’ He (the narrator) has said, ‘It had a back support, a narrow middle part and two straps in their front part (to go between the toes). He (the Imam) said, ‘It is the model of the footgear of the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause.’”
